purpose: ERV electrification thesis watchlist. Source of truth for ~/bin/x-ingest signal collector. The collector reconciles this against the matching private X List at run start.
_comment_list_id: Populated once by `x-ingest --setup` after creating the private X List of all 26 members. Required for the steady-state delta path (GET /2/lists/{id}/tweets). Backfill works without it.

accounts.name: Nat Bullard
accounts.relevance_note: Independent energy/transition analyst, runs Sightlines newsletter. Best data viz on transition pace; X carries his real-time annotations and hot-takes. Newsletter is primary source; X is supplement.

accounts.name: Hannah Ritchie
accounts.relevance_note: Our World in Data, climate/energy. Data-led, methodologically careful, frequently surfaces under-noticed trend lines. Site/newsletter primary; X is shortform commentary.

accounts.name: Jesse Jenkins
accounts.relevance_note: Princeton ZERO Lab. US grid/transmission/IRA policy, Wright's-Law thinking. X is his primary public venue for hot takes between papers. High signal-per-tweet.

accounts.name: Rewiring America
accounts.relevance_note: Org account substituted for personal handle (@SaulGriffith inactive). Electrification advocacy, residential heat-pump / induction / EV adoption messaging. US policy-relevant.

accounts.name: Michael Liebreich
accounts.relevance_note: BNEF founder, podcast host (Cleaning Up). Energy transition macro, hydrogen-skeptic stance, useful contrarian-but-rigorous voice on capex pace and tech maturity.

accounts.name: Ramez Naam
accounts.relevance_note: Climate futurist, ex-Microsoft, learning-curve evangelist. Strong signal for solar/battery/EV cost-curve milestones and 'when does X cross over' framing.

accounts.name: Auke Hoekstra
accounts.relevance_note: TU Eindhoven researcher. Methodologically rigorous EV/transition debunks. Catches bad analysis from oil/gas-funded studies. Essential for sanity-checking incoming claims.

accounts.name: Mateo Jaramillo
accounts.relevance_note: Form Energy CEO (multi-day iron-air storage). Industry-insider POV on long-duration storage market, grid economics, customer-side reality.

accounts.name: Jigar Shah
accounts.relevance_note: DOE Loan Programs Office director, ex-SunEdison founder, ex-Generate Capital. Project-finance signals for US clean energy at scale. Politics + capital flows.

accounts.name: Casey Handmer
accounts.relevance_note: Climate engineer, Terraform Industries founder. Concrete decarbonization tech (synfuels, direct-air capture, solar overbuild). Thinks in W/m^2 and learning-curves.

accounts.name: Shayle Kann
accounts.relevance_note: Latitude Media co-founder, Catalyst podcast host. Climate-tech analyst-investor, surfaces under-known startups and category dynamics. Sharp on what's overhyped vs under-hyped.

accounts.name: Javier Blas
accounts.relevance_note: Bloomberg commodities columnist. Oil/gas/LNG-side perspective on transition. Essential counterweight to electrons-only voices; surfaces real-world fossil incumbency dynamics.

accounts.name: Doomberg
accounts.relevance_note: Anonymous energy/markets Substack with contrarian POV (often nuclear-bullish, transition-skeptical). Substack is primary; X is teaser/commentary.

accounts.name: David Roberts
accounts.relevance_note: Volts podcast/newsletter. Deep US energy policy expert, especially grid/electrification/heat-pumps. Newsletter primary; X carries hot reactions to policy news.

accounts.name: Cody Simms
accounts.relevance_note: MCJ Collective, climate-tech VC and podcast. Sees deal flow signals, who's raising / what categories are hot. Investment-side intel.

accounts.name: Andrew Beebe
accounts.relevance_note: Obvious Ventures partner (climate-tech). Long-horizon investor perspective; comments on portfolio category dynamics.

accounts.name: John Kemp
accounts.relevance_note: Veteran oil/energy analyst (ex-Reuters, now independent). Quantitative, data-led commentary on inventories, prices, supply/demand. Useful for incumbent-fuels framing.

accounts.name: Rory Johnston
accounts.relevance_note: Commodity Context newsletter (oil markets). Newsletter primary; X carries inventory/spread observations.

accounts.name: Eli Dourado
accounts.relevance_note: Growth/abundance/infrastructure policy. Useful for hyperscale-grid / permitting / regulatory-as-bottleneck framing. Often crosses electrification with AI compute demand.

accounts.name: Andy Karsner
accounts.relevance_note: Alphabet's X (formerly DOE Asst Secretary for Energy Efficiency under Bush). Big-picture energy-transition thinker; surfaces moonshot/innovation signals.

accounts.name: Costa Samaras
accounts.relevance_note: Carnegie Mellon engineering prof, focus on energy/transport. Academic rigor on emissions accounting, transport electrification, climate-resilient infrastructure.

accounts.name: Latitude Media
accounts.relevance_note: Climate-tech media company (Shayle Kann's). Tracks for digest-only — content also reaches us through individual writer accounts in T1/T2.

accounts.name: BloombergNEF
accounts.relevance_note: Energy/transition research shop. Headline data drops (annual outlooks, sector reports). Institutional voice, weekly digest cadence sufficient.

accounts.name: International Energy Agency
accounts.relevance_note: Global energy reference body. Report releases and macro forecasts. Institutional voice, weekly digest cadence sufficient.

accounts.name: Columbia University Center on Global Energy Policy
accounts.relevance_note: Academic energy policy center. Workshop announcements, working-paper drops, conference signal.

accounts.name: Oxford Institute for Energy Studies
accounts.relevance_note: UK academic energy research. Gas/oil/LNG-specific scholarly output. Reaches us through individual researcher accounts in their own posts.

_dropped_v1.reason: Personal handle inactive on X. Substituted with @rewiringamerica org account (T1).
Moved to Bluesky. Drop until cross-platform support added.

Account inactive (0 tweets, 0 followers). May reactivate; revisit later.
